financials.margin_note: Operating margin (53.2%) excludes CapEx and savings — standard practice. All-in margin is 39.8%, still ~10x the industry average of 4.0%.

signals.name: Net Margin Outperformance
signals.summary: Elite Builds is running a 53.2% operating margin vs the industry average of 4.0%. That's a 49.2 percentage point gap.
signals.what_it_means: Firm is absorbing 15% material inflation and still generating strong profit. Peers cannot say the same.
signals.rm_action: Position as a low-risk credit client. Strong margin justifies preferential lending terms or a pre-approved working capital line.

signals.name: Inventory Cost Control Under Inflation
signals.summary: Inventory spend: $30,800. Pre-inflation equivalent would have been $26,783. Inflation premium absorbed: $4,017. Purchases split between 2 repeat vendors — likely on negotiated terms, not spot rates.
signals.what_it_means: Vendor relationships are acting as a partial hedge against the 15% material surge. Competitors buying at spot are paying more.
signals.rm_action: A supply chain credit facility would strengthen their procurement position further. Pitch it as a competitive tool, not a rescue product.

signals.name: Controlled Expense Velocity
signals.summary: Operating expenses are 46.8% of revenue. 100.0% of all expense transactions happen within 7 days of a client payment. Average gap: 3.2 days. Spending follows revenue, not the other way around.
signals.what_it_means: Banks are flagging 'High Expense Velocity' as a credit risk trigger. Elite Builds has the opposite profile.
signals.rm_action: Use this as underwriting evidence. This firm should be carved out of any blanket construction-sector credit tightening.

signals.name: Collect-First Payment Model
signals.summary: First client payment arrived 2026-03-01, before first inventory purchase on 2026-03-02. Pattern is: collect → then spend. Not the other way around.
signals.what_it_means: This firm runs on client deposits or milestone payments. No reliance on vendor credit to fund operations. That eliminates a whole category of liquidity risk.
signals.assumption: Inferred from timing patterns — not confirmed contractually.
signals.rm_action: Frame any credit product as a growth tool, not a cash-flow rescue. They don't need it to survive — they'd use it to scale.

signals.name: Liquidity Discipline — Active Savings
signals.summary: $5,000 moved to savings this month — 8.4% of net operating income. Distressed firms drain reserves. This firm is building them.
signals.what_it_means: Management is being deliberate about capital allocation during an industry downturn. That's a behavioural signal as much as a financial one.
signals.rm_action: Pitch a higher-yield business savings or money market account. Also useful for credit underwriting — shows buffer capacity.

signals.name: Implied Cash Reserves from Interest Income
signals.summary: Interest income of $120 observed. Back-calculating at 3.5% p.a. implies ~$41,143 in liquid reserves.
signals.what_it_means: A firm earning interest has idle cash. That's the opposite of a distressed business running on overdraft.
signals.assumption: Rate assumed at 3.5% p.a. Treat implied balance as directional, not exact.
signals.rm_action: ~$41,143 in liquid reserves confirms debt service capacity for any lending conversation.

signals.name: Capital Reinvestment Rate
signals.summary: $10,000 spent on new equipment (downpayment) — 8.9% of revenue. Healthy industry range is 5.0%–10.0%. Elite Builds is right in the middle.
signals.what_it_means: Distressed firms cut CapEx first. This firm is still investing in future capacity — expansion behaviour, not survival behaviour.
signals.assumption: Single-month snapshot. Ideally tracked across a full year for a reliable annualised rate.
signals.rm_action: The downpayment signals an active equipment purchase. Offer an asset-backed term loan or SBA 7(a) to complete it and preserve cash.

signals.name: Diversified Project Pipeline
signals.summary: 4 client payments received in a single month, spread across 26 days. Average gap between payments: 8.7 days. Largest single project is 40.2% of revenue — no dangerous concentration.
signals.what_it_means: Distressed firms show 1–2 clients and stalling pipelines. Elite Builds has a flowing order book with varied contract sizes.
signals.rm_action: Pipeline diversification is a strong underwriting positive. A revolving credit facility tied to project flow would be a natural fit.
